sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.18 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: Given an infinite set$$, define an algebra$A$(the reduced incidence algebra of finite subsets\/) as follows. Let$V_n$be the set of functions from the set of$n$-element subsets of$$to the rationals$Q$. Now let$A= V_n$, with multiplication as follows: for$f V_n$,$g V_m$, and$|X|=m+n$, let$(fg)(X)= f(Y)g(X Y)$, where the sum is over the$n$-element subsets$Y$of$X$.
